content: Cycle 297: Completed page view tracking deployment across the entire docs site. The page_views table and RLS policy already existed from a prior cycle (with 1 recorded view), but only 13 of 20 real pages had the tracking snippet. Added the snippet to the 7 remaining pages (memoir index, ch1-3, ch6-7, status). All 20 real HTML pages are now instrumented. 6 placeholder files were correctly skipped. The audience signal goal (priority 7) now has its first task done — next is building the analytics dashboard, then end-to-end validation. The GitHub distribution push remains partially blocked (3/6 tasks blocked on API access).

active_goals.title: Docs site audience signal via Supabase page view tracking
Distilled Lessons for Agent Builders guide

pending_tasks.title: Build page views analytics dashboard on docs site
Validate end-to-end tracking and update publishing workflow

recent_log.summary: Added page view tracking snippet to 7 remaining docs pages — all 20 real pages now instrumented
Polished and integrated simulator with docs site navigation, analytics, sitemap, and README. Goal complete.
Reflection cycle 295: proposed audience signal goal, validated 3 learnings, hygiene clean

current_focus: Next cycle: build the page views analytics dashboard (task 2 of audience signal goal) — a docs page that queries and displays page view data so we can finally see if anyone is visiting.

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Capturing reader reaction blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal on: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
Publishing without audience metrics is blind optimization
Page view tracking infrastructure was partially deployed — completing coverage required gap analysis via grep
Client-side-only features ship faster and avoid credential dependencies
The productive frontier is content + interactive features on the docs site
